source_coverage.notes: Social media (X/Twitter, Telegram, Discord, Slack) not fetchable via web_fetch; all web-accessible sources attempted

new_findings.title: CVE-2026-25643: Frigate NVR ≤0.16.3 Blind RCE via go2rtc Exec Injection
new_findings.description: Critical configuration manipulation vulnerability in Frigate NVR versions up to 0.16.3. By injecting a malicious go2rtc stream and fake camera entry into the configuration, an attacker can trigger arbitrary command execution as the Frigate process upon service restart. Both authenticated and unauthenticated exploitation paths exist. Public PoC available on GitHub.
new_findings.recommendation: Upgrade Frigate NVR to version > 0.16.3. Restrict access to Frigate API endpoints.

new_findings.title: CVE-2026-0770: Langflow Remote Code Execution via Code Validation Endpoint
new_findings.description: RCE vulnerability in Langflow's /api/v1/validate/code endpoint. When AUTO_LOGIN=true and default credentials are enabled, attackers can bypass authentication and execute arbitrary system commands through unsafe Python code evaluation during validation. Full server compromise possible.
new_findings.recommendation: Disable AUTO_LOGIN, change default credentials, restrict access to validation endpoints.

new_findings.title: CVE-2026-21962: New PoC Published (Ashwesker)
new_findings.description: New exploit PoC published by Ashwesker for CVE-2026-21962. Repository appeared in the last 3 hours on GitHub. Details pending full analysis — repo returned 404 suggesting it may have been taken down or is private.

new_findings.title: CVE-2026-21876: OWASP ModSecurity CRS WAF Bypass
new_findings.description: WAF bypass vulnerability in OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set (CRS). A PoC with Docker container has been published demonstrating the bypass technique. This could allow attackers to evade WAF protections and deliver malicious payloads to backend applications.
new_findings.recommendation: Monitor CRS project for patch. Review custom WAF rules.

new_findings.title: Wormable XMRig Cryptojacking Campaign with BYOVD Exploit and Time-Based Logic Bomb
new_findings.description: Sophisticated multi-stage cryptojacking campaign using pirated software bundles as lures to deploy a customized XMRig miner. Features worm-like capabilities spreading via external storage devices (enabling air-gapped lateral movement), BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) exploits for privilege escalation, and a time-based logic bomb. Reported by Trellix.
new_findings.affected: Windows systems, air-gapped environments via USB
new_findings.recommendation: Block known IoCs, disable autorun on removable media, monitor for suspicious driver loading.

new_findings.title: Linux Patch Wednesday February 2026: 632 Vulnerabilities Including New Critical RCEs
new_findings.description: February Linux Patch Wednesday addresses 632 vulnerabilities (305 in Linux Kernel). Notable new CVEs with public exploits: PyTorch RCE (CVE-2026-24747), Wheel RCE (CVE-2026-24049), Grafana EoP (CVE-2026-21721), Roundcube SFB (CVE-2026-25916), additional OpenSSL RCEs (CVE-2025-69421, CVE-2025-11187), pgAdmin RCEs, DiskCache RCE, Chromium SFB (CVE-2026-1504).
new_findings.affected: Linux distributions, PyTorch, Wheel, Grafana, Roundcube, pgAdmin, OpenSSL
new_findings.recommendation: Prioritize patching based on asset exposure. Focus on RCE vulnerabilities with public exploits.

new_findings.title: CVE-2026-2406: Telnet Vulnerability Analysis and Detection Tool
new_findings.description: New vulnerability in Telnet service with PoC and detection tooling published on GitHub. Appears related to the broader telnetd vulnerability class alongside CVE-2026-24061.
new_findings.recommendation: Disable Telnet in favor of SSH. If Telnet required, apply patches immediately.

new_findings.title: Blesta Billing Platform Multiple Vulnerabilities (KIS-2026-01/02/03)
new_findings.description: Multiple vulnerabilities disclosed in Blesta billing/hosting platform via Full Disclosure mailing list (KIS-2026-01, KIS-2026-02, KIS-2026-03). Details published on seclists.org.
new_findings.recommendation: Review Blesta vendor advisories and patch.

new_findings.title: Solax Power WiFi Module Vulnerabilities (SEC Consult)
new_findings.description: SEC Consult disclosed vulnerabilities in Solax Power WiFi modules used in solar inverter systems. Published on Full Disclosure mailing list.
new_findings.affected: Solax Power WiFi modules / solar inverter systems
new_findings.recommendation: Review SEC Consult advisory. Isolate IoT/OT devices from corporate networks.

new_findings.title: Remote BitLocker Bypass via Intel AMT
new_findings.description: Research published on Full Disclosure demonstrating remote BitLocker bypass using Intel Active Management Technology (AMT). Could allow physical security bypass on enterprise laptops with AMT enabled.
new_findings.affected: Systems with Intel AMT and BitLocker encryption
new_findings.recommendation: Review Intel AMT configuration. Ensure AMT is properly secured or disabled if not needed.

new_findings.title: CVE-2026-26717: Richie LMS Timing Attack Vulnerability
new_findings.description: Timing attack vulnerability discovered in Richie LMS, developed by France Université Numérique (OpenFUN). Could allow enumeration or authentication bypass through timing side-channel analysis.
new_findings.recommendation: Apply constant-time comparison for authentication operations.

dedup_filtered: OpenClaw security issues - reported multiple cycles

key_trends: AI/ML infrastructure vulnerabilities continue: Langflow RCE, Frigate NVR go2rtc injection
Linux ecosystem sees 632 CVEs in February patch cycle with 56+ public exploits
Wormable cryptojacking with BYOVD and air-gap jumping capability
WAF bypass techniques evolving (ModSecurity CRS bypass)
IoT/OT security: solar inverter WiFi modules, NVR systems under scrutiny
